arts.ttl: Arne Slot offers hugely exciting Liverpool update ahead of Milan test
arts.sum: One of the classic Champions League ties will take place on Tuesday evening, as Liverpool take on Milan at San Siro. It will be Arne Slot’s first chance to see how well his Reds side fare against the best that the continent have to offer, and with the Rossoneri beginning their Serie A campaign with […] The post Arne Slot offers hugely exciting Liverpool update ahead of Milan test appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: 'Quite extraordinary!' - Liverpool's biggest ever win, 50 years ago today - Liverpool FC
arts.sum: I was a defender and I only scored 13 goals in total for Liverpool, so getting two in one game for me was quite extraordinary!” Phil Thompson, more renowned for keeping the opposition out at the other end of the pitch, was on the scoresheet twice, and he was joined by Phil Boersma (also two), Alec Lindsay, Emlyn Hughes, Steve Heighway, Tommy Smith, Peter Cormack, Ray Kennedy and Ian Callaghan.

arts.ttl: Liverpool manager Slot: “We’re not afraid of Milan, Chiesa not ready to start”
arts.sum: Liverpool manager Arne Slot addressed the media ahead of his team’s Champions League opener against AC Milan. Speaking to Sky Sport and in a press conference, the Dutch coach shared his thoughts on the upcoming match and the state of his squad. Reflecting on Milan’s history, Slot said, “The Dutch players from Milan’s past were legends even before they went to Italy, and after as well. At that time, we Dutch followed Milan because of these players. That’s why Milan is a special club.” Regarding team selection, Slot emphasized the need for careful consideration: “Every match is different. arts.sum: Liverpool have at least quarter of their Champions League matches during the League phase of the competition on Amazon's Prime Video streaming service rather than TNT Sports

arts.ttl: Milan vs Liverpool: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: It's Arne Slot's Champions League debut as Liverpool head coach and the Dutchman will want to see significant improvements from Saturday's 1-0 home defeat to Nottingham Forest. Liverpool and Milan have plenty of history from the noughties, with two Champions League final encounters, but their most recent meetings came in 2021/22.

arts.ttl: The fixture which shows football’s changing face - and how AC Milan got left behind
arts.sum: The Rossoneri and Liverpool met in the finals of 2005 and 2007; in 2024 they meet in the opening fixture of a brand new Champions League format
